1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}
38	
39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
44		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
45		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
46		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
47	}
48	
49	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
50	type BeadEvent struct {
51		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
52		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
53		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
54		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
55		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
56		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
57	}
58	
59	// Status constants
60	const (
61		StatusOpen       = "open"
62		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
63		StatusClosed     = "closed"
64		// StatusBlocked marks an explicit external blocker that can be rechecked.
65		// Dependency waits are derived from dependency status and do not persist as
66		// blocked.
67		StatusBlocked = "blocked"
68		// StatusProposed marks work awaiting operator approval or clarification.
69		// It is outside worker dispatch until transitioned to open or cancelled.
70		StatusProposed = "proposed"
71		// StatusCancelled marks work that will not run and does not satisfy
72		// dependents.
73		StatusCancelled = "cancelled"

105		// IssueTypeOperatorPrompt is the bead type used for operator-submitted
106		// prompts that the execute-loop runs as instructions.
107		IssueTypeOperatorPrompt = "operator-prompt"
108	)

110	// Default labels and other defaults for operator-prompt beads.
111	const (
112		OperatorPromptLabelKind   = "kind:operator-prompt"
113		OperatorPromptLabelSource = "source:web-ui"
114		// OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance is the auto-AC stub used when an
115		// operator submits a prompt without explicit acceptance criteria. The
116		// structural AC verifier is skipped for operator-prompt beads, so this
117		// stub stands as a human-readable contract only.
118		OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance = "Agent must produce a diff or no_changes rationale; the prompt body is the contract."
119	)
120	
121	// OperatorPromptMutationGuard enforces the no-self-mutation rule from
122	// Story 15: an operator-prompt bead's execution may not create, edit, or
123	// close another operator-prompt bead. The guard returns nil when the
124	// mutation is allowed and a non-nil error when it must be rejected.
125	//
126	// actorIssueType is the issue_type of the bead currently being executed
127	// (empty when no operator-prompt context is active). targetIssueType is
128	// the issue_type of the bead about to be mutated.
129	//
130	// Allow/deny matrix:
131	//
132	//	actor=""               , target=*                → allow
133	//	actor="task"           , target=*                → allow
134	//	actor="operator-prompt", target!="operator-prompt" → allow
135	//	actor="operator-prompt", target=="operator-prompt" → deny
136	func OperatorPromptMutationGuard(actorIssueType, targetIssueType string) error {
137		if actorIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
138			return nil
139		}
140		if targetIssueType != IssueTypeOperatorPrompt {
141			return nil
142		}
143		return fmt.Errorf("bead: operator-prompt bead may not mutate another operator-prompt bead")
144	}

146	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
147	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
148	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
149	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
150	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
151	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
152	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.
153	//
154	// The returned bead is not persisted; callers feed it to Store.Create which
155	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
156	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
157		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)
158		title := body
159		if i := strings.IndexByte(body, '\n'); i >= 0 {
160			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
161		}
162		if title == "" {
163			title = "(empty operator prompt)"
164		}
165		tier := defaultTier
166		if tier < MinPriority {
167			tier = MinPriority
168		}
169		if tier > MaxPriority {
170			tier = MaxPriority
171		}
172		return &Bead{
173			Title:       title,
174			IssueType:   IssueTypeOperatorPrompt,
175			Status:      StatusProposed,
176			Priority:    tier,
177			Labels:      []string{OperatorPromptLabelKind, OperatorPromptLabelSource},
178			Description: body,
179			Acceptance:  OperatorPromptDefaultAcceptance,
180		}
181	}

190		MaxPriority     = 4

241	// DepIDs returns a flat list of dependency IDs for this bead.
242	func (b *Bead) DepIDs() []string {
243		var ids []string
244		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
245			ids = append(ids, d.DependsOnID)
246		}
247		return ids
248	}
249	
250	// HasDep returns true if the bead depends on the given ID.
251	func (b *Bead) HasDep(id string) bool {
252		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
253			if d.DependsOnID == id {
254				return true
255			}
256		}
257		return false
258	}
259	
260	// AddDep adds a dependency if it doesn't already exist.
261	func (b *Bead) AddDep(depID, depType string) {
262		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
263			if d.DependsOnID == depID {
264				return // already exists
265			}
266		}
267		b.Dependencies = append(b.Dependencies, Dependency{
268			IssueID:     b.ID,
269			DependsOnID: depID,
270			Type:        depType,
271			CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
272		})
273	}
274	
275	// RemoveDep removes a dependency by target ID.
276	func (b *Bead) RemoveDep(depID string) {
277		var filtered []Dependency
278		for _, d := range b.Dependencies {
279			if d.DependsOnID != depID {
280				filtered = append(filtered, d)
281			}
282		}
283		b.Dependencies = filtered
284	}

76	// ResolveTriagePolicy returns the effective triage policy for this config,
77	// layering any `triage.policies` entries on top of the binary default
78	// ladders. Unknown failure-mode names and unknown action names are
79	// silently dropped (schema validation surfaces typos at load time).
80	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveTriagePolicy() triage.TriagePolicy {
81		policy := triage.DefaultPolicy()
82		if c == nil || c.Triage == nil || len(c.Triage.Policies) == 0 {
83			return policy
84		}
85		for modeStr, actionStrs := range c.Triage.Policies {
86			mode, err := triage.ParseFailureMode(modeStr)
87			if err != nil {
88				continue
89			}
90			ladder := make([]triage.Action, 0, len(actionStrs))
91			for _, a := range actionStrs {
92				act, err := triage.ParseAction(a)
93				if err != nil {
94					continue
95				}
96				ladder = append(ladder, act)
97			}
98			if len(ladder) == 0 {
99				continue
100			}
101			policy.Ladders[mode] = ladder
102		}
103		return policy
104	}

8	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.
9	// Only actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim; intake_error fail-opens.
10	type PreClaimIntakeOutcome string
11	
12	const (
13		PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_atomic"
14		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
15		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
16		PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"
17		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
18	)
19	
20	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
21	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
22	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
23		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
24		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
25		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`
26		Detail       string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
27		Rewrite      PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
28		// Decomposition is populated when Outcome == too_large_decomposed and the
29		// hook produced a concrete split with children and AC map.
30		Decomposition *PreClaimDecomposition `json:"decomposition,omitempty"`
31	}
32	
33	// PreClaimDecompositionChild is one proposed child bead in a splitter result.
34	type PreClaimDecompositionChild struct {
35		Title       string   `json:"title"`
36		Description string   `json:"description"`
37		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance"`
38		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	// ACMapEntry maps one parent acceptance criterion to child coverage.
42	// Coverage must be non-empty; use "operator_required" or "non_scope" for
43	// ACs that cannot be expressed as a child bead.
44	type ACMapEntry struct {
45		ParentAC string `json:"parent_ac"` // text of the parent AC
46		Coverage string `json:"coverage"`  // child AC text | "operator_required" | "non_scope"
47	}
48	
49	// PreClaimDecomposition is the splitter output when outcome is too_large_decomposed.
50	// Every parent AC must have a corresponding ACMap entry with non-empty Coverage;
51	// a split with missing or empty Coverage entries is lossy and blocks for operator.
52	type PreClaimDecomposition struct {
53		Children  []PreClaimDecompositionChild `json:"children"`
54		ACMap     []ACMapEntry                 `json:"ac_map"`
55		Rationale string                       `json:"rationale"`
56	}
57	
58	// isDecompositionLossy returns true if any ACMapEntry has empty Coverage,
59	// indicating the split does not fully cover all parent acceptance criteria.
60	func isDecompositionLossy(acMap []ACMapEntry) bool {
61		for _, entry := range acMap {
62			if strings.TrimSpace(entry.Coverage) == "" {
63				return true
64			}
65		}
66		return false
67	}
68	
69	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
70	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
71	// claim.
72	type PreClaimIntakeRewrite struct {
73		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
74		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
75		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
76	}
77	
78	// PreClaimIntakeHook classifies a ready bead before Claim. The loop treats
79	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
80	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
81	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)
82	
83	func (r PreClaimIntakeResult) normalizedOutcome() PreClaimIntakeOutcome {
84		switch r.Outcome {
85		case "", PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
86			return PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
87		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
88			PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, PreClaimIntakeError:
89			return r.Outcome
90		default:
91			return PreClaimIntakeError
92		}
93	}

13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

12	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadArchiveCommand() *cobra.Command {
13		cmd := &cobra.Command{
14			Use:   "archive",
15			Short: "Archive closed beads when active beads.jsonl exceeds size threshold",
16			Long: `Move closed beads from .ddx/beads.jsonl into .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl.
17	
18	By default this command archives closed beads only, and only when the active
19	beads.jsonl is larger than ` + fmt.Sprintf("%d", bead.DefaultArchiveSizeThreshold) + ` bytes (4MB). Each archived bead's inline
20	events are externalized into its attachment sidecar at
21	.ddx/attachments/<id>/events.jsonl.
22	
23	Flags override the trigger:
24	  --max-size N      change the size threshold (use 0 to disable the gate)
25	  --older-than D    only archive beads whose last update is older than D
26	  --max-count N     cap the number of beads moved in this run
27	
28	The operation is crash-safe: archive-partner row writes precede active-row
29	removal, and merged-view reads in 'ddx bead show' / 'ddx bead list' hide any
30	duplicate that an interrupted run could leave behind.`,
31			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				s := f.beadStore()
34	
35				threshold := bead.DefaultArchiveSizeThreshold
36				if cmd.Flags().Changed("max-size") {
37					v, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt64("max-size")
38					threshold = v
39				}
40	
41				info, statErr := os.Stat(s.File)
42				activeSize := int64(0)
43				if statErr == nil {
44					activeSize = info.Size()
45				}
46	
47				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
48	
49				if threshold > 0 && activeSize <= threshold {
50					if asJSON {
51						enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())
52						enc.SetIndent("", "  ")
53						return enc.Encode(map[string]any{
54							"EventsExternalized": 0,
55							"Archived":           0,
56							"ActiveSizeBefore":   activeSize,
57							"ActiveSizeAfter":    activeSize,
58							"Threshold":          threshold,
59							"Skipped":            true,
60						})
61					}
62					fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(),
63						"Active beads.jsonl is %d bytes (≤ threshold %d); nothing to archive.\n",
64						activeSize, threshold)
65					return nil
66				}
67	
68				policy := bead.ArchivePolicy{
69					Statuses: []string{bead.StatusClosed},
70				}
71				if cmd.Flags().Changed("older-than") {
72					d, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("older-than")
73					policy.MinAge = d
74				}
75				if cmd.Flags().Changed("max-count") {
76					n, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-count")
77					policy.BatchSize = n
78				}
79	
80				stats, err := s.ArchiveWithEvents(policy)
81				if err != nil {
82					return err
83				}
84	
85				afterSize := int64(0)
86				if info, err := os.Stat(s.File); err == nil {
87					afterSize = info.Size()
88				}
89	
90				if stats.Changed() {
91					if _, err := f.beadAutoCommit(fmt.Sprintf("archive: externalize=%d archive=%d", stats.EventsExternalized, stats.Archived)); err != nil {
92						return err
93					}
94				}

96				if asJSON {
97					enc := json.NewEncoder(cmd.OutOrStdout())

99					return enc.Encode(map[string]any{

16	func (s *Store) runHook(name string, b *Bead) error {
17		hookPath := filepath.Join(s.Dir, "hooks", name)
18	
19		info, err := os.Stat(hookPath)
20		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
21			return nil // no hook installed
22		}
23		if err != nil {
24			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook stat: %w", err)
25		}
26		if info.IsDir() {
27			return nil
28		}
29		// Check executable
30		if info.Mode()&0o111 == 0 {
31			return nil // not executable
32		}
33	
34		data, err := marshalBead(*b)
35		if err != nil {
36			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook marshal: %w", err)
37		}
38	
39		cmd := exec.Command(hookPath)
40		cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader(data)
41		var stderr bytes.Buffer
42		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
43	
44		runErr := cmd.Run()
45		if runErr == nil {
46			return nil // exit 0 = ok
47		}
48	
49		exitErr, ok := runErr.(*exec.ExitError)
50		if !ok {
51			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook %s: %w", name, runErr)
52		}
53	
54		msg := strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String())
55		if msg == "" {
56			msg = "validation failed"
57		}
58	
59		switch exitErr.ExitCode() {
60		case 1:
61			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook %s: %s", name, msg)
62		case 2:
63			// Warning — print but don't block
64			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: hook %s warning: %s\n", name, msg)
65			return nil
66		default:
67			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook %s exit %d: %s", name, exitErr.ExitCode(), msg)
68		}

8	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.

11

14		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
15		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
16		PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"
17		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
18	)
19	
20	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
21	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
22	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
23		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
24		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
25		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`
26		Detail       string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
27		Rewrite      PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
28		// Decomposition is populated when Outcome == too_large_decomposed and the
29		// hook produced a concrete split with children and AC map.
30		Decomposition *PreClaimDecomposition `json:"decomposition,omitempty"`
31	}
32	
33	// PreClaimDecompositionChild is one proposed child bead in a splitter result.
34	type PreClaimDecompositionChild struct {
35		Title       string   `json:"title"`
36		Description string   `json:"description"`
37		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance"`
38		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	// ACMapEntry maps one parent acceptance criterion to child coverage.
42	// Coverage must be non-empty; use "operator_required" or "non_scope" for
43	// ACs that cannot be expressed as a child bead.
44	type ACMapEntry struct {
45		ParentAC string `json:"parent_ac"` // text of the parent AC
46		Coverage string `json:"coverage"`  // child AC text | "operator_required" | "non_scope"
47	}
48	
49	// PreClaimDecomposition is the splitter output when outcome is too_large_decomposed.
50	// Every parent AC must have a corresponding ACMap entry with non-empty Coverage;
51	// a split with missing or empty Coverage entries is lossy and blocks for operator.
52	type PreClaimDecomposition struct {
53		Children  []PreClaimDecompositionChild `json:"children"`
54		ACMap     []ACMapEntry                 `json:"ac_map"`
55		Rationale string                       `json:"rationale"`
56	}
57	
58	// isDecompositionLossy returns true if any ACMapEntry has empty Coverage,
59	// indicating the split does not fully cover all parent acceptance criteria.
60	func isDecompositionLossy(acMap []ACMapEntry) bool {
61		for _, entry := range acMap {
62			if strings.TrimSpace(entry.Coverage) == "" {
63				return true
64			}
65		}
66		return false
67	}
68	
69	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
70	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
71	// claim.
72	type PreClaimIntakeRewrite struct {
73		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
74		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
75		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
76	}
77	
78	// PreClaimIntakeHook classifies a ready bead before Claim. The loop treats
79	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
80	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
81	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)
82	
83	func (r PreClaimIntakeResult) normalizedOutcome() PreClaimIntakeOutcome {
84		switch r.Outcome {
85		case "", PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
86			return PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
87		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
88			PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, PreClaimIntakeError:
89			return r.Outcome
90		default:
91			return PreClaimIntakeError
92		}
93	}

305	}

307	// AgentConfig represents agent service configuration in .ddx/config.yaml

310		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models,omitempty" json:"models,omitempty"`

313		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir,omitempty" json:"session_log_dir,omitempty"`
314		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions,omitempty" json:"permissions,omitempty"`
315		Endpoints       []AgentEndpoint     `yaml:"endpoints,omitempty" json:"endpoints,omitempty"`
316		Routing         *RoutingConfig      `yaml:"routing,omitempty" json:"routing,omitempty"`

321	// TriageConfig controls the bead readiness gate decomposition depth.
322	type TriageConfig struct {
323		// MaxDecompositionDepth caps recursive bead splitting. Children re-enter
324		// the gate; at cap the parent is blocked with label=needs-human-decomposition.
325		// Zero or unset uses the binary default (3). Configurable as
326		// agent.triage.max_decomposition_depth in .ddx/config.yaml.
327		MaxDecompositionDepth *int `yaml:"max_decomposition_depth,omitempty" json:"max_decomposition_depth,omitempty"`
328	}

330	// ResolveMaxDecompositionDepth returns the effective depth cap for the triage
331	// gate. Defaults to 3 when unset or non-positive.
332	func (c *NewConfig) ResolveMaxDecompositionDepth() int {
333		const defaultDepth = 3
334		if c == nil || c.Agent == nil || c.Agent.Triage == nil || c.Agent.Triage.MaxDecompositionDepth == nil {
335			return defaultDepth
336		}
337		if *c.Agent.Triage.MaxDecompositionDepth <= 0 {
338			return defaultDepth
339		}
340		return *c.Agent.Triage.MaxDecompositionDepth

343	// AgentEndpoint describes one endpoint-first native agent provider target.

355		// Increase this for local thinking models that need >15 min to produce

440	// DefaultNewConfig returns a new config with default values applied
441	func DefaultNewConfig() *NewConfig {
442		return &NewConfig{
443			Version: "1.0",
444			Library: &LibraryConfig{
445				Path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
446				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
447					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
448					Branch: "main",
449				},
450			},
451			PersonaBindings: make(map[string]string),
452			UpdateCheck: &UpdateCheckConfig{
453				Enabled:   true,
454				Frequency: "24h",
455			},
456		}
457	}
458	
459	// DefaultConfig is an alias for DefaultNewConfig for compatibility
460	var DefaultConfig = DefaultNewConfig()
461	
462	// GetMetaPrompt returns the meta-prompt path, defaulting to focused.md if unset
463	// Returns empty string if explicitly set to null/empty (disabled)
464	func (c *NewConfig) GetMetaPrompt() string {
465		if c.System == nil || c.System.MetaPrompt == nil {
466			// Unset: return default
467			return "claude/system-prompts/focused.md"
468		}
469		// Explicitly set (could be empty string to disable)
470		return *c.System.MetaPrompt
471	}
472	
473	// ApplyDefaults ensures all required fields have default values
474	func (c *NewConfig) ApplyDefaults() {
475		if c.Version == "" {
476			c.Version = "1.0"
477		}
478		if c.Library == nil {
479			c.Library = &LibraryConfig{
480				Path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
481				Repository: &RepositoryConfig{
482					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
483					Branch: "main",
484				},
485			}
486		} else {
487			if c.Library.Path == "" {
488				c.Library.Path = ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
489			}
490			if c.Library.Repository == nil {
491				c.Library.Repository = &RepositoryConfig{
492					URL:    "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library",
493					Branch: "main",
494				}
495			} else {
496				if c.Library.Repository.URL == "" {
497					c.Library.Repository.URL = "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library"
498				}
499				if c.Library.Repository.Branch == "" {
500					c.Library.Repository.Branch = "main"
501				}
502			}
503		}
504		if c.Bead == nil {
505			c.Bead = &BeadConfig{}
506		}
507		if c.PersonaBindings == nil {
508			c.PersonaBindings = make(map[string]string)
509		}
510		if c.UpdateCheck == nil {
511			c.UpdateCheck = &UpdateCheckConfig{
512				Enabled:   true,
513				Frequency: "24h",
514			}
515		} else {
516			if c.UpdateCheck.Frequency == "" {
517				c.UpdateCheck.Frequency = "24h"
518			}
519		}
520		if c.Executions == nil {
521			c.Executions = &ExecutionsConfig{RetainDays: 90}
522		} else if c.Executions.RetainDays == 0 {
523			c.Executions.RetainDays = 90
524		}
525	}

1	package cmd
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"fmt"
6		"os"
7		"path/filepath"
8	
9		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead/accheck"
10		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
11	)
12	
13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

package agent

import (
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newACQualityTestStore(t *testing.T, acceptance string) (*bead.Store, *bead.Bead) {
	t.Helper()
	root := t.TempDir()
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(root, ".ddx"))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	b := &bead.Bead{
		ID:         "ddx-acq-001",
		Title:      "ac quality test bead",
		IssueType:  bead.DefaultType,
		Status:     bead.StatusOpen,
		Priority:   2,
		Acceptance: acceptance,
		Extra:      map[string]any{"execution-eligible": true},
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
	return store, b
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_AllMechanicalACs_Passes(t *testing.T) {
	// All ACs are test-name and build-gate: score = 1.0 → above threshold
	acceptance := "1. TestPreClaimACQuality_AllMechanicalACs_Passes\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n3. lefthook run pre-commit passes"
	store, b := newACQualityTestStore(t, acceptance)

	eligible, err := CheckACQuality(context.Background(), store, b.ID, 0.5)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, eligible, "all mechanical ACs must pass the quality gate")

	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// execution-eligible must not be set to false when passing
	assert.NotEqual(t, false, got.Extra["execution-eligible"])
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_AllProseACs_BlocksClaim(t *testing.T) {
	// All ACs are prose: score = 0.0 → below threshold → blocks
	acceptance := "1. improve the output clarity\n2. logs become cleaner and more readable\n3. users find the interface easier to understand"
	store, b := newACQualityTestStore(t, acceptance)

	eligible, err := CheckACQuality(context.Background(), store, b.ID, 0.5)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, eligible, "all-prose ACs must block claim")

	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, false, got.Extra["execution-eligible"])
	assert.Contains(t, got.Labels, labelACQualityNeedsRefinement)
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreAboveThreshold_Passes(t *testing.T) {
	// 3 mechanical + 1 prose = 0.75 → above 0.5 default threshold → passes
	acceptance := "1. TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreAboveThreshold_Passes\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green\n3. lefthook run pre-commit passes\n4. output looks better overall"
	store, b := newACQualityTestStore(t, acceptance)

	eligible, err := CheckACQuality(context.Background(), store, b.ID, 0.5)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, eligible, "mixed ACs above threshold must pass the quality gate")
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreBelowThreshold_Routes(t *testing.T) {
	// 1 mechanical + 3 prose = 0.25 → below 0.5 default threshold → routes
	acceptance := "1. TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreBelowThreshold_Routes\n2. improve overall output\n3. output is noticeably better\n4. users appreciate the change"
	store, b := newACQualityTestStore(t, acceptance)

	eligible, err := CheckACQuality(context.Background(), store, b.ID, 0.5)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, eligible, "mixed ACs below threshold must route to refinement")

	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, false, got.Extra["execution-eligible"])
	assert.Contains(t, got.Labels, labelACQualityNeedsRefinement)
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_LowQualityEmitsACQualityEvent(t *testing.T) {
	// Low-quality ACs must emit an "ac-quality-low" event with per-AC
	// classifications in the body.
	acceptance := "1. improve the output clarity\n2. logs become cleaner"
	store, b := newACQualityTestStore(t, acceptance)

	_, err := CheckACQuality(context.Background(), store, b.ID, 0.5)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	events, err := store.Events(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var found bool
	var qualityEvent bead.BeadEvent
	for _, ev := range events {
		if ev.Kind == "ac-quality-low" {
			found = true
			qualityEvent = ev
			break
		}
	}
	require.True(t, found, "ac-quality-low event must be emitted for low-quality ACs")

	var body map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(qualityEvent.Body), &body))
	assert.Contains(t, body, "score")
	assert.Contains(t, body, "threshold")
	assert.Contains(t, body, "items")
	assert.Contains(t, body, "verifiable_count")
	assert.Contains(t, body, "prose_count")
	items, ok := body["items"].([]any)
	require.True(t, ok, "items must be a JSON array")
	assert.Equal(t, 2, len(items), "event body must carry one entry per AC")
	// Each item must carry kind classification.
	for _, raw := range items {
		item, ok := raw.(map[string]any)
		require.True(t, ok)
		assert.Contains(t, item, "kind")
		assert.Contains(t, item, "text")
		assert.Contains(t, item, "ac")
	}
}

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadLint_CLI_PrintsClassification(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	rootCmd := factory.NewRootCommand()

	// Create a bead with mixed AC quality: 2 mechanical + 1 prose.
	acceptance := "1. TestBeadLint_CLI_PrintsClassification\n2. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... green\n3. output looks better overall"
	createOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "create", "AC lint test bead",
		"--acceptance", acceptance)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	beadID := strings.TrimSpace(createOut)
	require.NotEmpty(t, beadID)

	// Human-readable output must include score and AC classifications.
	out, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "lint", beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "score:")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "test-name")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "build-gate")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "prose")

	// JSON output must include bead_id, score, and items array.
	jsonOut, err := executeCommand(rootCmd, "bead", "lint", beadID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var result map[string]any
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(jsonOut), &result))
	assert.Equal(t, beadID, result["bead_id"])
	assert.Contains(t, result, "score")
	assert.Contains(t, result, "verifiable_count")
	assert.Contains(t, result, "prose_count")
	assert.Contains(t, result, "total")
	assert.Contains(t, result, "items")
	items, ok := result["items"].([]any)
	require.True(t, ok, "items must be a JSON array")
	assert.Equal(t, 3, len(items), "one entry per AC")
}

data.text:
All pre-commit hooks pass and the commit lands clean. All 6 required tests pass, the full test suite is green, and lefthook passes.

**Summary of changes:**

- **`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_ac_quality.go`** (in checkpoint): `ScoreACQuality` classifies ACs via `accheck.ParseAcceptance` and returns a verifiability score (non-prose / total). `CheckACQuality` applies the gate: when score < threshold it sets `Extra["execution-eligible"]=false`, adds `"ac-quality:needs-refinement"` label, and emits an `"ac-quality-low"` event with per-AC classifications.

- **`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_ac_quality_test.go`** (in checkpoint): All 5 `TestPreClaimACQuality_*` tests covering all/none/mixed AC scenarios and event emission.

- **`cli/internal/config/types.go`**: Added `Intake *IntakeConfig` to `NewConfig`, `IntakeConfig`/`IntakeACQualityConfig` types, and `ResolveIntakeACQualityMinScore()` defaulting to 0.5.

- **`cli/cmd/bead_lint.go`**: `ddx bead lint <id>` subcommand printing per-AC kind classifications and verifiability score, with `--json` flag.

- **`cli/cmd/bead_lint_test.go`**: `TestBeadLint_CLI_PrintsClassification` covering both human and JSON output.

- **`cli/cmd/bead.go`**: Registered `f.newBeadLintCommand()`.
